title: Extract Jinja macro for polling section wrappers in dashboard index.html
analysis: index.html repeats an identical HTMX polling-section wrapper pattern 5 times (orchestrators, performance, memory, memory-graphs, recon). Each instance contains: an outer <div>, a <section> with data-section/hx-get/hx-trigger/hx-swap/hx-request/aria-live attributes, skeleton placeholder content, and a <div data-updated-for=NAME> timestamp indicator. The data-section and data-updated-for values are implicitly coupled — base.html JavaScript (lines 46-90) queries data-updated-for by the data-section name to update timestamps and show error states. The macro makes this coupling explicit by deriving both from a single 'name' parameter. The codebase already has a macro file (macros/badges.html) and comprehensive macro tests (test_badge_macros.py) establishing clear patterns: FileSystemLoader-based Jinja env, helper render functions, class-grouped test methods asserting on HTML attributes/classes. The new macro uses Jinja's caller() mechanism for skeleton content, keeping each section's unique skeleton markup at the call site while extracting all boilerplate.

steps.description: Write test_polling_macro.py with failing tests for the polling_section macro: (1) section element has data-section=name, (2) section has hx-get attribute, (3) section has hx-trigger attribute, (4) section has hx-request with timeout JSON, (5) section has hx-swap='morph:innerHTML' (constant), (6) section has aria-live='polite' (constant), (7) data-updated-for div has matching name, (8) data-updated-for div has aria-hidden='true', (9) data-updated-for div has expected CSS classes, (10) caller block content is rendered inside the section, (11) outer wrapper div is present. Follow test_badge_macros.py patterns: module-scoped jinja_env fixture, helper render function using caller block syntax.

Create macros/polling_section.html with the polling_section(name, hx_get, hx_trigger, hx_request_timeout) macro. Uses caller() for skeleton content. Renders: outer <div>, <section> with data-section, hx-get, hx-trigger, hx-swap='morph:innerHTML', hx-request JSON timeout, aria-live='polite', then caller() content, then </section>, then <div> with data-updated-for=name, timestamp CSS classes, aria-hidden='true'.

Add structural tests to test_polling_macro.py verifying index.html imports and uses the macro: (1) index.html contains the polling_section import, (2) index.html contains 5 call blocks for each section name, (3) each call passes correct hx_get/hx_trigger/timeout parameters. These tests will fail because index.html hasn't been refactored yet.

Refactor index.html to import polling_section from macros/polling_section.html and replace all 5 repeated wrapper patterns with {% call polling_section(...) %}...skeleton...{% endcall %} blocks. Preserve each section's unique skeleton HTML as the caller block content. Verify all 5 sections: orchestrators (10s, 8000), performance (30s, 12000), memory (10s, 8000), memory-graphs (60s, 10000), recon (15s, 12000).

Add an equivalence test verifying that the macro-rendered output for each of the 5 sections contains the critical attributes that base.html JavaScript depends on (data-section, data-updated-for matching, hx-get paths). This serves as a regression guard for the JS coupling documented in base.html lines 46-90.

Run full test suite for dashboard (existing tests + new tests) to confirm no regressions. Fix any issues found. The refactored index.html must produce semantically identical HTML output for all 5 sections.

design_decisions.decision: Place macro in a separate file macros/polling_section.html rather than adding to macros/badges.html
design_decisions.rationale: badges.html is focused on inline badge/dot rendering. A polling section wrapper is structurally different (block-level, uses caller()). Separate files match the single-responsibility pattern and make imports self-documenting.

design_decisions.decision: Use caller() block for skeleton content rather than a string parameter
design_decisions.rationale: Each section has unique skeleton HTML (varying heights, nested divs). caller() preserves the HTML naturally at the call site without escaping issues, matches Jinja best practices for block content.

design_decisions.decision: Keep hx-swap and aria-live as constants inside the macro rather than parameters
design_decisions.rationale: All 5 sections use identical values (morph:innerHTML, polite). Making these parameters would add complexity without value. If a future section needs different values, the macro can be extended then.

design_decisions.decision: Derive hx-get default from name (e.g. /partials/{name}) but accept explicit override
design_decisions.rationale: Currently all 5 sections follow the pattern hx-get='/partials/{name}'. Keeping it as an explicit parameter (per task spec) maintains flexibility without magic, but all current usages happen to follow this convention.

design_decisions.decision: Include a docstring comment block in the macro file matching badges.html convention
design_decisions.rationale: badges.html opens with a {# ... #} block documenting all macros. Consistency aids discoverability for future contributors.

reuse.what: Jinja macro test pattern from test_badge_macros.py
reuse.how: Reuse the same approach: FileSystemLoader-based jinja_env fixture (module-scoped), helper render functions that build inline template strings importing the macro, assertion-based HTML attribute checking. The jinja_env fixture definition is identical.

reuse.what: Macro file structure and docstring convention from badges.html
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: {# docstring block #} at top describing the macro, then {% macro ... %} definition. Same indentation style.

reuse.what: Structural import test pattern from TestTemplateMacroImports
reuse.how: Reuse the pattern of reading template file content and asserting on import strings to verify index.html properly imports the new macro.
